AXIA
1. Purpose & Vision

1. Purpose & Vision

1.1 Product Vision

AXIA is a human-centric AI-powered insider risk platform designed to help security, legal, and leadership teams understand, investigate, and resolve risk events involving people and sensitive data.

AXIA does not replace enforcement systems (DLP, email gateways, CASB). AXIA observes, explains, contextualizes, and guides human action.

Lead Tagline: "The CISO's Front-Line AI for Insider Risk"
Supporting Taglines:
- "Context. Engagement. Resolution." — captures the three core pillars
- "WHO sent it. WHAT it contains. WHO received it." — the differentiating framework
AXIA
1.2 The Problem AXIA Solves

1.2 The Problem AXIA Solves

MetricIndustry Reality
Average DLP alerts per day1,000+
False positive rate70-90%
Time to investigate one alert15-45 minutes
Analyst burnout rate53% report burnout
Average time to contain insider incident86 days
Average cost per insider incident$16.2M

Legacy DLP tools are pattern-blind (see keywords, not intent), context-deaf (treat all users the same), action-limited (block or alert only), and deployment-heavy (6-12 months to tune).

AXIA
1.3 AXIA's Answer

1.3 AXIA's Answer

AXIA is the judgment and engagement layer that:

  1. Connects to existing tools (no rip-and-replace)
  2. Understands context DLP misses (role, behavior, relationships, intent)
  3. Judges true risk vs. noise (targeting <10% false positive rate after learning period)
  4. Recommends actions (engage, escalate, block)
  5. Learns continuously, making DLP smarter over time

The WHO / WHAT / WHO Framework

AXIA's core analytical framework is built on three questions:

WHO sent it. WHAT it contains. WHO received it.
That's how you find the right cases and assign the right severity.
Traditional DLPAXIA's Judgment
Pattern matching on content onlySemantic understanding of content
No understanding of sender contextRole, department, baseline behavior
No understanding of recipient relationshipRecipient trust level and history
Binary: block or allowContextual severity with explanation

This framework is the foundation of AXIA's differentiation: while DLP tools focus only on WHAT the data is, AXIA understands WHY it matters based on WHO is sending it and WHO is receiving it.

Figure: WHO / WHAT / WHO Framework — AXIA's core analytical differentiator combining sender context, content analysis, and recipient context for judgment-based severity scoring.

AXIA
1.4 Competitive Positioning

1.4 Competitive Positioning

AXIA occupies a unique position no competitor has claimed:

AXIA DifferentiatorCompetitive Status
"Understands WHY something is risky"Unclaimed
"Judgment and engagement layer"Unclaimed
Human-in-loop insider riskUnclaimed
Orchestrates decision-makingUnclaimed

vs. Traditional DLP (Symantec, Forcepoint, Purview): AXIA adds semantic understanding, behavioral analysis, and active engagement. DLP does pattern matching; AXIA understands situations.

vs. Insider Risk Platforms (DTEX, Code42, Proofpoint ITM): AXIA adds deep content analysis and proactive intervention. IRM detects; AXIA prevents and coaches.

vs. AI-Native DLP (MIND, Orion): AXIA adds human engagement and intent understanding. MIND automates blocking; AXIA judges and engages humans in decisions.

vs. Cloud Security (Netskope, Zscaler): AXIA focuses on people and intent, not network traffic. AXIA enhances tools; doesn't replace them.

AXIA
1.5 Name Etymology

1.5 Name Etymology

AXIA derives from Greek "axios" (worthy/valuable) — the root of "axiology" (the study of value and judgment). AXIA determines what's worth investigating.
AXIA

2. Target Users & Personas

2.1 Primary Users

User PersonaPrimary UseKey Screens
CISO / Head of SecurityStrategic oversight, trend analysis, executive reporting, posture managementInsights, Posture, Intel
Insider Risk / SOC AnalystDay-to-day investigation, case management, case resolutionCommand, Case Details, Intel
Legal & ComplianceEvidence review, regulatory compliance, audit trailCommand, Case Details, Settings
HR / ManagementContext review for employee situations (read-only or guided)Case Details (limited)
Executive LeadershipOversight, trends, board reportingInsights, Posture
Security AdministratorConfiguration, integrations, policy managementConnect, Enforce, Settings

AXIA
3. Product Principles

3. Product Principles

3.1 Core Principles

Brand Principles (from Brand Strategy V3)

These five brand principles govern all AXIA visual and verbal identity:

PrincipleDescription
Vigilant, Not AnxiousOur visuals convey calm alertness. We're always watching, but never panicking. Dark, focused interfaces with purposeful motion.
Judgment Over EnforcementWe don't just detect, we decide. Our brand reflects intelligence and discernment, not just protection.
Human-Centered AITechnology that coaches rather than blocks. Our visual language is warm enough to feel approachable, professional enough to trust.
Clarity in ComplexitySecurity is complex. Our design makes it comprehensible. Clean hierarchy, clear signals, actionable insights.
Trusted Advisor PresenceWe speak like a trusted advisor in the room, confident but not arrogant, direct but not aggressive.

Product Principles

  1. AXIA explains — humans decide. Every AI output must be explainable in plain language.
  2. People-first language. No accusatory framing. Focus on data protection, not surveillance.
  3. Context over volume. 80 cases > 8,000 alerts. Quality over quantity.
  4. Investigation before visualization. Textual analysis is primary; charts are secondary.
  5. Deploy fast, protect immediately. Hours, not months.
  6. Insights lead to action. Every recommendation is actionable.
  7. Judgment over enforcement. Coaching over blocking preserves relationships.
AXIA
3.2 The Four Positioning Pilla...

3.2 The Four Positioning Pillars

Aligned with Positioning & Messaging Framework V3

PillarSubtitleWhat It Means
CONTEXT"Context is King"AXIA understands the full picture: WHO sent it, WHAT it contains, WHO received it. Role context, behavioral baselines, data sensitivity, recipient trust, and timing signals combine to determine the RIGHT severity.
ENGAGES"Not just alerts"AXIA doesn't just notify your SOC. It coaches the careless, alerts managers, and escalates intelligently. Engagement drives real behavior change, not just incident logging.
RESOLVES"Cases, not queues"Every case moves toward resolution. Not just triaged, not just acknowledged, but actually resolved with a full audit trail. Cases have outcomes; behavior changes; risk reduces.
GUIDES"Guides Security Systems"AXIA doesn't replace your DLP. It learns from resolved cases and provides intelligence that helps your security systems make better decisions, adjusting and optimizing enforcement based on learned patterns.
"Context. Engagement. Resolution." — The three core pillars, with GUIDES as the enabling layer that makes the others work.
AXIA
3.3 Key Messages

3.3 Key Messages

Eight differentiating points from Brand Strategy V3 that define AXIA's unique value:

#MessageProduct Alignment
1Context > Static RulesAXIA adds behavioral context: sender's role, content semantics, and recipient trust to assign the RIGHT severity. Maps to CONTEXT pillar and WHO/WHAT/WHO framework.
2Filling the GapsAXIA catches what static rules miss AND validates what they flag. Context distinguishes legitimate activity from real risk. Maps to AXIA-Only Detection cases.
3AXIA Guides Security SystemsAXIA learns from resolved cases and provides intelligence that helps DLP systems make better decisions. Maps to GUIDES pillar and Enforce tab.
4Engagement > AlertsAlerts alone aren't enough. AXIA engages: orchestrates conversations, tracks resolution, drives real outcomes. Maps to ENGAGES pillar and engagement workflows.
5Pre-Departure ProtectionCorrelates HR data (resignation) with behavioral data (access spikes). DLP cannot do this alone. Maps to departure risk cases.
6Posture TransformationRisk Control Score improves over time. Cases actually get resolved, not just logged. Maps to RESOLVES pillar and Posture tab.
7Cross-Department CoverageProtects the entire organization. Cases across Engineering, Sales, Finance, HR, Legal, and Ops. Maps to Command tab case diversity.
8Human-Centric ResponseCoaching over blocking. Employee engagement preserves relationships and gets to root cause. Maps to "Judgment Over Enforcement" brand principle.
AXIA
3.4 Brand & Design Reference

3.4 Brand & Design Reference

For full details, see Brand Strategy V3 (AXIA/Marketing/Branding/branding-presentation-v3.html).

Recommended Brand Direction: "Intelligent Watchguard" — Teal + Cyan, AI-powered vigilance.

Logo Direction: Scanning Lens (primary), Connector Hub (backup). Both align with "Intelligent Watchguard" positioning.

Color System:

RoleColorHex
Primary: Deep TealDeep Teal#09434e
Secondary: TealTeal#0D9488
Accent: CyanCyan#22D3EE
Optional Warm AccentBurnt Orange#B8540C

UI Direction: Dark-first design for SOC operations. Deep teal base works in dark mode; reduces eye strain in dim SOC environments. The demo and product are already built dark-first. Light mode is supported as optional.

Motion Principles: "Vigilant but not anxious" — purposeful, quick, smoothly damped.

AXIA
4. Release Phases & Scope

4. Release Phases & Scope

AXIA ships in three phases, each expanding AI processing depth, case handling automation, and screen scope. Engineering should build P1 as a complete, shippable product. P2 and P3 add capabilities that require accumulated data and operational maturity.

AXIA
4.1 Phase Overview

4.1 Phase Overview

PhaseAI ProcessingCase HandlingScreens In Scope
P1Single interaction (email)AI recommends, operator approvesCommand, Cases, Case Details, Intel, Connect, Settings
P2Multiple interactions per userAutomated sending (configurable)+ Insights, + Posture
P3Cross-organization patternsOrchestration (email threads, auto-close)+ Enforce (smart blocks)

Figure: Release Phase Timeline — Three-phase delivery cadence showing screen scope, AI capability progression, and case handling automation evolution.

AXIA
4.2 P1 Scope — Single Interact...

4.2 P1 Scope — Single Interaction, AI-Recommended Actions

P1 delivers a complete investigation platform for single-interaction analysis with AI-powered recommendations that require human approval.

In Scope:

CapabilityDescription
Command tabCase investigation workbench — operational triage hub with severity cards and active case prioritization
Cases tabFull case repository with advanced search, filtering, bulk operations, and saved searches
Case DetailsFull investigation with structured analysis, AI recommendations, and manual actions
Intel tabConversational AI advisor, limited to single-interaction context
Connect tabData source management and connection health
Settings tabConfiguration, AI boundaries, org guidance; automation toggles visible but greyed out with "Available in P2" label
AI EngineFull structured analysis per email (What Happened / Why It Matters / Why It Is Unusual) — no behavioral baseline cross-referencing
AI-Generated Engagement RecommendationsFor each case, AI recommends next steps with draft communication language based on criticality, reasoning, and allowed actions from Settings. All five engagement types (Employee Coaching, Manager Notification, Legal Escalation, HR Notification, Evidence Preservation) get AI-generated drafts. Operator must manually review, edit, and approve before any action executes.
Manual case actionsAssign, close, acknowledge, escalate — all analyst-initiated after reviewing AI recommendations

Out of Scope for P1:

CapabilityPhaseRationale
Insights tab (analytics dashboard)P2Requires accumulated data for meaningful trends
Enforce tab (DLP integration view, smart block policies)P3Requires organizational intelligence + enforcement loop
Posture tab (risk posture overview)P2Requires trend data and resolved-case history
Automated engagement executionP2System sending emails without operator approval requires trust built during P1
Behavioral baseline comparisonP2Requires multi-interaction data accumulation
Cross-user / cross-org pattern detectionP3Requires organizational-level data corpus

AI in P1:

  • Analyzes each email independently using LLM semantic analysis
  • Generates full What / Why It Matters / Why It Is Unusual structure per interaction
  • "Why It Is Unusual" derives context from role heuristics, recipient novelty, timing anomalies, and volume indicators from directory/HR data — NOT from historical behavioral baselines (which are P2)
  • Generates recommended next steps and draft engagement language (coaching email, manager briefing, legal escalation, HR notification, evidence preservation) — all require manual operator approval
  • Risk scoring based on content semantics + sender role context + recipient context (from directory/HR data), but NOT from historical behavioral patterns
  • Intel queries limited to individual case context (no trend analysis, no "show me all cases for this user")
AXIA
4.3 P2 Scope — User Behavioral...

4.3 P2 Scope — User Behavioral Analysis, Automated Engagement

P2 adds behavioral intelligence and engagement automation. Requires P1 deployment with accumulated interaction data.

Adds to P1:

CapabilityDescription
Insights tabCurated analytics dashboard with fixed-layout widgets telling the AXIA value story
Posture tabRisk posture score, severity distribution, trend comparison
Behavioral baselinesAI processes multiple interactions per user to build individual and role-based behavioral baselines
Automated engagementAutomation toggles in Settings become active. System can auto-send engagement communications based on configured severity rules. No longer requires manual approval for configured scenarios.
Enhanced IntelUser-level analysis ("show me this person's risk history"), trend queries, cross-case pattern detection per user
Enhanced risk scoringRisk scoring now includes behavioral deviation from individual and role baselines
"Why It Is Unusual" upgradeNow includes historical behavioral deviation context alongside P1's role/directory heuristics
AXIA
4.4 P3 Scope — Organizational ...

4.4 P3 Scope — Organizational Intelligence, Orchestration

P3 adds cross-organizational intelligence and full case orchestration. Requires mature behavioral baselines from P2.

Adds to P2:

CapabilityDescription
Enforce tabDLP integration view with smart block policies. Resolved cases inform DLP policy adjustments.
Cross-org pattern detectionAI analyzes patterns across the organization (department-level, cross-team risk concentrations)
Case orchestrationSystem manages ongoing email conversations, auto-follows-up on unresolved coaching outreach, auto-closes cases when employee confirms compliance
Enforcement loopContinuous learning: resolved cases → enforcement policy recommendations → DLP policy adjustments
Enhanced IntelOrg-level analysis ("which departments are trending high-risk?"), policy effectiveness queries
AXIA
4.5 AI Response Time Requireme...

4.5 AI Response Time Requirements

AI response times are benchmarked at approximately 2x public LLM response times for comparable complexity. These targets supersede any conflicting latency specifications elsewhere in this document.

OperationPhaseTargetBenchmark
Single email risk scoringP1< 6 seconds~2x ChatGPT simple query
Structured analysis generation (What/Why/Unusual)P1< 20 seconds~2x ChatGPT detailed response
Engagement draft generationP1< 10 seconds~2x ChatGPT message drafting
Intel conversational queryP1< 10 seconds~2x ChatGPT conversation
Document analysis (Intel upload)P1< 60 seconds~2x ChatGPT document analysis
User behavioral analysisP2< 30 secondsAggregation over multiple interactions
Org-level pattern detectionP3< 120 secondsCross-organization computation
AXIA
4.6 Phase Transition: P1 → P2

4.6 Phase Transition: P1 → P2

When P2 ships for existing P1 customers:

  • Insights and Posture tabs appear in navigation
  • Settings automation toggles become active (previously greyed out)
  • "Why It Is Unusual" analysis begins incorporating behavioral baselines for new cases
  • Existing P1 cases are NOT retroactively re-scored; behavioral baselines apply to new cases only
  • First-run tooltip or notification explains new capabilities when user first sees new tabs
AXIA

5. Platform Architecture & Operational Model

5.1 Five-Stage Operational Model
