6. User Stories & Acceptance C...

6. User Stories & Acceptance Criteria

Phase Summary

PhaseUser Stories
P1US-001, US-002, US-003, US-004, US-005, US-006, US-011, US-012, US-013, US-015, US-016, US-017, US-018, US-019, US-020 (Cases tab), US-021 (Cases tab)
P2US-007, US-008, US-009, US-010
P3US-014
AXIA
6.1 Command Tab — Case Investi...

6.1 Command Tab — Case Investigation

US-001: View and Filter Cases (P1)

As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to view a filtered list of risk cases with severity, status, and DLP tags so that I can prioritize my investigation queue.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Command tab loads case list within 2 seconds
  • ☐ Severity filter allows multi-select (Critical/High/Medium/Low) with color indicators
  • ☐ Status filter allows multi-select (Open/Closed)
  • ☐ DLP system filter shows connected DLP names (Purview/Netskope)
  • ☐ Time range selector (1d/7d/30d/90d) updates case list
  • ☐ Active filters show as removable chips below filter bar
  • ☐ "Clear all" removes all filters in one click
  • ☐ Case count updates dynamically with filters
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to search cases using natural language so that I can find relevant incidents without learning query syntax.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Search bar accepts natural language (e.g., "Critical cases involving Engineering")
  • ☐ Results update within 3 seconds
  • ☐ Search works across case title, entity name, department, description
  • ☐ Empty results show helpful suggestions
  • ☐ Search can be combined with active filters
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to click a case card to see full details so that I can investigate and decide on actions.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Clicking case card navigates to Case Details screen
  • ☐ Back button returns to Command with filters preserved
  • ☐ Breadcrumb shows Command > CASE-XXXX
  • ☐ Case Details loads within 3 seconds
AXIA
6.1A Cases Tab — Case List & M...

6.1A Cases Tab — Case List & Management

US-020: Search and Filter Full Case Repository (P1)

As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to search and filter across all cases (open and closed) with advanced filters so that I can find specific cases, identify patterns, and manage my case inventory.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Cases tab loads full case list within 2 seconds (up to 500 cases)
  • ☐ AI-assisted search works identically to Command tab search
  • ☐ Structured search mode allows field-based queries (entity name, case ID, department, date range)
  • ☐ Advanced filters include: severity, status, DLP system, time range, department, entity, action taken, assigned to
  • ☐ Filters can be saved and recalled as "Saved Searches"
  • ☐ Active filters show as removable chips above case list
  • ☐ Case list supports column sorting (click header to sort/reverse)
  • ☐ Pagination supports 25/50/100 cases per page
  • ☐ Row density toggle (compact/comfortable) available
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to select multiple cases and perform bulk operations so that I can efficiently manage my case workload.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Checkbox selection allows selecting individual cases or "select all on page"
  • ☐ Bulk assign: assign selected cases to a specific analyst
  • ☐ Bulk close: close selected cases with a shared resolution reason
  • ☐ Bulk export: export selected cases to CSV or PDF
  • ☐ Confirmation dialog shows count of selected cases before bulk operation
  • ☐ Bulk operations appear in Activity Timeline for each affected case
AXIA
6.2 Case Details — Investigati...

6.2 Case Details — Investigation & Action

US-004: Read Structured Analysis (P1)

As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to see structured analysis (What Happened / Why It Matters / Why Unusual) so that I can make an informed decision without reading raw content.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Three analysis sections are clearly labeled with icons
  • ☐ What Happened includes person, asset type, channel, destination
  • ☐ Why It Matters includes business impact assessment
  • ☐ Why Unusual derives context from role heuristics, recipient novelty, timing anomalies, and volume indicators from directory/HR data (P1) — NOT from historical behavioral baselines (P2)
  • ☐ DLP Outcome shows Blocked/Alerted/Not Detected state
  • ☐ Each section has "Ask Intel" link for deeper analysis
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to review AI-recommended actions with draft communications, edit them if needed, and approve them so that I can handle cases efficiently while maintaining a full audit trail.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Actions section appears only for Open cases
  • ☐ Actions grouped by category (Engagement/Investigation)
  • ☐ AI-recommended actions are pre-checked based on severity playbook and case criticality
  • ☐ Each recommended action includes an explanation of why it is recommended (tied to the What/Why/Unusual analysis)
  • ☐ "View Generated Drafts" button shows AI-generated draft communications for each engagement action (coaching email, manager briefing, legal escalation, HR notification, evidence preservation memo)
  • ☐ Operator can review and edit AI-generated drafts inline before approving
  • ☐ "Confirm Selected Actions (N)" button shows count of selected actions
  • ☐ Confirmation triggers execution (e.g., sends the approved email)
  • ☐ Audit trail records: original AI recommendation, AI-generated draft, operator edits (if any), final approved version, execution timestamp
  • ☐ Confirmed actions appear in Activity Timeline with attribution (AI-recommended + operator-approved)
  • ☐ Actions are immutable once confirmed
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to preview source content and view documents with sensitive sections highlighted so that I can understand what triggered the case.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Source Content section is expandable/collapsible
  • ☐ Email metadata (From/To/Subject) displayed
  • ☐ Content preview shows truncated body
  • ☐ Attachments show filename, type, size, DLP status
  • ☐ Double-clicking attachment opens Document Viewer
  • ☐ Document Viewer highlights sensitive sections with color coding
  • ☐ Hover over highlight shows tooltip explaining sensitivity
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to click an entity name to see their full risk profile so that I can understand behavioral patterns across cases.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Entity name is clickable in Case Details
  • ☐ Clicking navigates to Intel with pre-populated entity query
  • ☐ Intel response includes role, department, tenure, prior cases, behavioral summary
  • ☐ Back navigation returns to Case Details
  • ☐ Entity name is clickable in Case Details
  • ☐ Clicking navigates to Intel with pre-populated entity query
  • ☐ Intel response includes role, department, tenure from directory/HR data, and the current case analysis — but does NOT include prior cases or behavioral summary (which require P2 multi-interaction data)
  • ☐ Intel clearly indicates: "Behavioral history available in a future update" or similar
AXIA
6.3 Insights — Analytics & Rep...

6.3 Insights — Analytics & Reporting (P2)

US-008: View Risk Control Score Over Time (P2)

As a CISO, I want to see a composite risk posture score over time so that I can report organizational insider risk health to the board.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Risk Control Score widget shows current score prominently
  • ☐ Area chart shows score trend over selected time period
  • ☐ 90-day view shows improvement arc (baseline to current)
  • ☐ Hover shows score composition breakdown
  • ☐ Chart uses real date labels (month names)
As a CISO, I want to see DLP alert volume compared to true alerts so that I can demonstrate AXIA's false positive reduction value.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Signal vs. Noise widget shows dual-area chart
  • ☐ Upper area represents total DLP alerts
  • ☐ Lower area represents AXIA-verified true alerts
  • ☐ Noise reduction percentage prominently displayed
  • ☐ 90-day view shows dramatic noise reduction improvement
As a CISO, I want to see risk distributed by department and severity so that I can identify organizational risk concentrations.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Heatmap shows departments as rows, severity levels as columns
  • ☐ Color intensity reflects case count
  • ☐ Hover shows exact count
  • ☐ Time selector updates distribution
AXIA
6.4 Intel — Conversational AI

6.4 Intel — Conversational AI

US-011: Query Risk Patterns via Intel (P1)

As a CISO, I want to ask natural language questions about risk patterns so that I can get strategic insights without running manual reports.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Intel accepts text queries up to 4,000 characters
  • ☐ Response renders markdown (tables, lists, emphasis)
  • ☐ Suggested prompts appear as clickable chips
  • ☐ Response includes actionable buttons where appropriate
  • ☐ Response latency < 5 seconds
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to upload a document and specify sender/recipient context so that Intel can assess its risk level.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ File upload supports PDF, DOCX, TXT, CSV, XLSX, XLS, PPT, PPTX, MD, JSON
  • ☐ Max file size 20MB with clear error for oversized files
  • ☐ Optional sender/recipient fields available during upload
  • ☐ AI response includes sensitivity classification, content categories, findings
  • ☐ Response latency < 30 seconds for document analysis
As an Insider Risk Analyst, I want to navigate to Intel from a case to ask case-specific questions.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ "Ask Intel About This Case" button in Case Details
  • ☐ Intel loads with case context, shows "Discussing: CASE-XXXX"
  • ☐ Quick prompts change to case-specific questions
  • ☐ "New Conversation" clears case context
AXIA
6.5 Enforce — DLP Integration ...

6.5 Enforce — DLP Integration (P3)

US-014: View Connected DLP Systems (P3)

As a Security Administrator, I want to see all connected DLP systems with their integration status so that I can ensure enforcement coverage.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Each connected DLP shows name, scope, status, sync time
  • ☐ Available DLP systems shown with "Connect" action
  • ☐ Coming Soon DLP systems shown (grayed)
  • ☐ Stats show events ingested and correlation status
AXIA
6.6 Connect — Data Sources

6.6 Connect — Data Sources

US-015: Connect a New Data Source (P1)

As a Security Administrator, I want to connect a new data source so that AXIA can analyze its content for insider risk.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Available sources shown with "Connect" button
  • ☐ Connection flow includes OAuth/API key step
  • ☐ Permission review shows what data AXIA will access
  • ☐ Test connection verifies before completing
  • ☐ Progress indicator during initial sync
  • ☐ Newly connected source appears with green status
As a Security Administrator, I want to see the health status of all connected sources so that I can troubleshoot issues.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Each source shows Connected/Available/Error/Coming Soon state
  • ☐ Connected sources show last sync time and health indicator
  • ☐ Error state shows error description and troubleshooting steps
  • ☐ Clicking connected source expands to show detailed stats
AXIA
6.7 Settings — Configuration

6.7 Settings — Configuration

US-017: Configure AI Boundaries (P1)

As a CISO, I want to clearly see and configure what AXIA may and may not do autonomously so that I can trust the system.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ AI Boundaries section expanded by default
  • ☐ "May Suggest" items show green checkmarks
  • ☐ "May NEVER Do" items show red X icons with distinct styling
  • ☐ All statements use clear, non-technical language
  • ☐ Boundaries are enforceable (not just display)
As a Security Administrator, I want to upload company policies so that AXIA references them in case analysis.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Upload accepts PDF, DOCX, TXT up to 10MB
  • ☐ Uploaded docs show in list with name, date, size
  • ☐ AXIA references uploaded policies in case analysis
  • ☐ Delete requires confirmation
  • ☐ Upload errors show clear messages
As a CISO, I want to see automation capabilities that will be available in future phases so that I understand the platform's roadmap and what my organization is building toward.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • ☐ Settings displays engagement automation toggles (auto-send coaching, auto-notify manager, etc.) in a distinct "Engagement Automation" section
  • ☐ All automation toggles are visible but greyed out / disabled in P1
  • ☐ Each greyed-out toggle shows tooltip: "Available in P2" with brief description of what the automation will do
  • ☐ Clicking a greyed-out toggle does not change state but shows informational popover
  • ☐ When P2 ships, toggles become active with no UI migration required (same controls, now enabled)
AXIA
7. Navigation & Information Ar...

7. Navigation & Information Architecture

7.1 Tab Structure

The application uses a left-hand sidebar navigation. Tabs are released in phases:

TabLabelIconPurposePhase
1CommanddashboardCase investigation workbench — the operational triage hubP1
2Casesfolder_openCase list & filtering — full case repository and managementP1
3IntelpsychologyConversational AI advisor — strategic analysisP1
4ConnecthubData source connections and integration managementP1
5SettingssettingsConfiguration, AI boundaries, organizational guidanceP1
6InsightsinsightsAnalytics dashboard — performance and value metricsP2
7PosturesecurityRisk posture overview with severity distributionP2
8EnforceshieldDLP integration and monitoring, smart block policiesP3

P1 Navigation: Command | Cases | Intel | Connect | Settings P2 Navigation: Command | Cases | Insights | Intel | Posture | Connect | Settings P3 Navigation: Command | Cases | Insights | Intel | Enforce | Posture | Connect | Settings

Figure: Navigation Architecture by Phase — Sidebar wireframes showing how the navigation evolves from 5 tabs (P1) to 8 tabs (P3). Note: Cases tab was added post-diagram creation and is not visually shown in the sidebar wireframes above; it appears between Command and Intel in all phases.

Sub-screens (accessed via navigation within tabs):

  • Case Details — accessed from Command or Cases by clicking a case
  • Document Viewer — accessed from Case Details by double-clicking an attachment
AXIA
7.2 Breadcrumb Patterns

7.2 Breadcrumb Patterns

ScreenBreadcrumb
Command(none — home)
CasesCases
Case Details (from Command)Command > CASE-XXXX
Case Details (from Cases)Cases > CASE-XXXX
Document Viewer[parent] > CASE-XXXX > Document
InsightsInsights
IntelIntel
Intel (Case Context)[parent] > CASE-XXXX > Intel
EnforceEnforce
PosturePosture
ConnectConnect
SettingsSettings
AXIA
8. Command Tab — Case Investig...

8. Command Tab — Case Investigation Workbench

8.1 Intent

The Command tab is the primary operational screen. It provides a filtered, searchable case list that enables analysts to quickly identify, prioritize, and investigate risk cases.

Screenshot: Command Tab — Risk Command Center showing severity cards (Critical/High/Medium/Low), totals bar, and accordion-style case list with AI-generated case summaries.

AXIA
8.2 Header

8.2 Header

ElementDescription
Screen Title"Command"
Case CountDynamic count of visible cases (e.g., "Showing 14 cases")
RefreshWith last-sync timestamp
AXIA
8.3 Filter System

8.3 Filter System

Horizontal filter bar at the top of the case list:

FilterOptionsDefaultBehavior
SeverityCritical, High, Medium, LowAll selectedMulti-select dropdown with color indicators
StatusOpen, ClosedAll selectedMulti-select dropdown
DLP SystemPurview, Netskope, (other connected DLPs)All selectedMulti-select dropdown; shows which DLP system tagged the case
Time Range1d, 7d, 30d, 90d30dHorizontal button group, single-select

Filter UX:

  • Click filter button opens dropdown with checkboxes
  • Active filters show badge with count
  • Active state changes button styling (primary color background)
  • "Clear all" link removes all filters
  • Active filter tags display below filter bar as removable chips
AXIA
8.4 Case List

8.4 Case List

Full-width case cards with complete information:

ElementDescription
Severity BadgeColor-coded left border + severity label (Critical=red, High=orange, Medium=blue, Low=gray)
Risk ScoreNumeric score (0-100) in severity-colored badge
Case TitleHuman-readable headline describing the incident
TimestampRelative time (e.g., "23m ago") or absolute date
Entity RowName, Role, Department — name is clickable (navigates to Intel)
Description2-line truncated summary of the case
Status BadgeOpen (green) / Closed (gray)
DLP TagsPurview / Netskope badges showing which DLP systems are involved
Actions TakenBadge list of completed actions (e.g., "Employee Coached", "Manager Notified")

Card States:

  • Open: Full opacity, colored left border matching severity
  • Closed: 75% opacity, no colored border, hover restores opacity
  • Click anywhere on card navigates to Case Details
  • Entity name click navigates to Intel with pre-populated query
  • Cards support keyboard navigation (Tab/Enter)
AXIA
8.5 Load More / Pagination

8.5 Load More / Pagination

  • Shows "Showing X of Y cases" at bottom
  • "Load more cases" button for additional results
  • Lazy loading for performance
AXIA
8.6 AI-Assisted Search

8.6 AI-Assisted Search

ElementDescription
Search BarAI sparkle icon, placeholder: Try: "Critical cases involving Engineering"
BehaviorNatural language queries parsed by AI to filter/sort results
Examples"Critical cases involving Engineering this quarter", "Cases where DLP alerted", "Departing employees"

Figure: Case Investigation Flow — End-to-end user journey from DLP alert through case investigation to resolution, showing the Command-to-Case Details workflow.

AXIA
8A. Cases Tab — Case List & Fi...

8A. Cases Tab — Case List & Filtering

8A.1 Intent

The Cases tab provides a dedicated, full-featured case management view focused on searching, filtering, and managing the full case inventory. While the Command tab is the operational triage hub (designed for real-time prioritization of active cases), the Cases tab is the case repository where analysts can search across all cases (open and closed), apply advanced filters, perform bulk operations, and manage case lifecycle.

Screenshot: Cases Tab — Investigation workbench with AI-assisted search, multi-select severity/status/actions/DLP filters, and scrollable case list.

Phase: P1 — The Cases tab is part of P1 scope, providing comprehensive case management alongside the Command tab's triage focus.
AXIA
8A.2 Header

8A.2 Header

ElementDescription
Screen Title"Cases"
Total Case CountTotal cases matching current filters (e.g., "247 cases")
Quick Stats BarOpen count, Closed count, Critical count — clickable as quick filters
AXIA
8A.3 Search & Filter System

8A.3 Search & Filter System

The Cases tab provides more advanced search and filtering than the Command tab:

Search Bar

ElementDescription
AI-Assisted SearchAI sparkle icon, natural language queries (same engine as Command tab)
Structured SearchToggle to structured mode: field-based search (entity name, case ID, department, date range)
Saved SearchesSave and recall frequently used filter combinations

Filter Panel

FilterOptionsDefaultBehavior
SeverityCritical, High, Medium, LowAll selectedMulti-select with color indicators
StatusOpen, Closed, EscalatedAll selectedMulti-select dropdown
DLP SystemConnected DLP namesAll selectedMulti-select dropdown
Time RangeCustom date picker + presets (1d/7d/30d/90d/All)30dDate range selector
DepartmentOrganization departmentsAllMulti-select with search
EntitySearch by employee nameNoneType-ahead search
Action TakenEmployee Coached, Manager Notified, Legal Escalation, HR Notified, Evidence Preserved, No ActionAllMulti-select dropdown
Assigned ToAnalyst namesAllMulti-select dropdown

Filter UX:

  • Persistent filter panel on left side (collapsible)
  • Active filter summary shown as removable chips above case list
  • Filter counts update in real-time as filters are applied
  • "Reset all" clears all filters to defaults
  • Saved searches accessible via dropdown
AXIA
8A.4 Case List View

8A.4 Case List View

The case list in the Cases tab uses a table/list hybrid format optimized for scanning large numbers of cases:

ColumnDescriptionSortable
SeverityColor-coded badge (Critical/High/Medium/Low)Yes
Risk ScoreNumeric score (0-100)Yes
Case IDCASE-XXXXX format, clickable to Case DetailsYes
Case TitleHuman-readable headlineYes
EntityEmployee name + departmentYes
StatusOpen/Closed/Escalated badgeYes
DLP SourceWhich DLP system(s) flagged the caseNo
Actions TakenBadge list of completed actionsNo
Last UpdatedRelative or absolute timestampYes

List Interaction:

  • Click case row to navigate to Case Details
  • Checkbox selection for bulk operations
  • Column sorting (click header to sort, click again to reverse)
  • Adjustable column widths (drag column borders)
  • Row density toggle (compact/comfortable)
AXIA
8A.5 Bulk Operations

8A.5 Bulk Operations

OperationDescription
Bulk AssignAssign selected cases to an analyst
Bulk CloseClose selected cases with shared resolution reason
Bulk ExportExport selected cases to CSV/PDF
AXIA
8A.6 Pagination & Performance

8A.6 Pagination & Performance

ElementDescription
Pagination25/50/100 cases per page selector
Total Count"Showing 1-50 of 247 cases"
Infinite Scroll OptionToggle between pagination and infinite scroll
Performance TargetCase list renders within 2 seconds for up to 500 cases
AXIA
8A.7 Relationship to Command T...

8A.7 Relationship to Command Tab

The Command and Cases tabs serve complementary purposes:

AspectCommand TabCases Tab
Primary UseReal-time triage and prioritizationFull case repository and management
Default ViewRecent/active cases with severity cardsAll cases with advanced filtering
Interaction ModelBrowse and investigateSearch, filter, manage
Bulk OperationsNoYes
Saved SearchesNoYes
Advanced FiltersBasic (severity, status, DLP, time)Full (+ department, entity, action, assigned to)

Both tabs navigate to the same Case Details screen when a case is selected.

AXIA
9. Case Details Screen

9. Case Details Screen

9.1 Intent

Help a human decision-maker understand what happened and decide what to do. This is where AXIA's judgment becomes visible.

Screenshot: Case Details — AI Analysis — Structured three-part analysis (What Happened / Why It Matters / Why It Is Unusual) providing context-aware risk explanation for a critical IP exfiltration case.

AXIA
9.2 Header

9.2 Header

ElementDescription
Back ButtonReturns to Command with filters preserved
BreadcrumbCommand > CASE-XXXX
Case TitleFull title
Status IndicatorOpen (green pulsing dot) / Closed (gray check)
Severity IndicatorColor-coded badge with severity label
AXIA
9.3 Entity Information Card

9.3 Entity Information Card

Prominent card at the top of Case Details:

FieldDescription
AvatarInitials in colored circle
Full NameClickable — navigates to Intel with entity query
Role/Titlee.g., "Senior Software Engineer"
Departmente.g., "Engineering"
Join Datee.g., "Joined Jan 2020"
Risk ContextTags showing risk factors (e.g., "Resignation submitted", "Key project contributor", "Departure in 5 days")
AXIA
9.4 Structured Analysis (Core ...

9.4 Structured Analysis (Core Section)

Three-part analysis with icons — this is AXIA's primary value delivery:

Figure: AI Analysis Structure — The four-card stacked layout (What Happened / Why It Matters / Why It Is Unusual / DLP Outcome) that forms AXIA's core value delivery mechanism.

WHAT HAPPENED

  • Icon: visibility (filled)
  • Content: Concrete, factual description including person name, role, asset type, channel, destination (domain/external org), volume, timing
  • Link: "Ask Intel" — navigates to Intel with context: "What similar events has this person had?"

WHY IT MATTERS

  • Icon: warning (filled)
  • Content: Business and organizational impact: IP exposure, legal risk, competitive risk, regulatory concerns, financial risk
  • Link: "Ask Intel" — navigates to Intel with context: "What's our policy on this?"

WHY IT IS UNUSUAL

  • Icon: public (filled)
  • Content (P1): Role-based heuristic deviation: recipient novelty (first-time external domain), timing anomaly (off-hours, weekend), volume indicators (bulk data), role-context mismatch (engineer sending to competitor domain). Derived from directory/HR data and single-interaction metadata — NOT from historical behavioral baselines.
  • Content (P2+): Adds individual behavioral baseline comparison, historical pattern deviation, and peer-group deviation analysis.
  • Link: "Ask Intel" — navigates to Intel with context: "What's normal for this role?"

DLP OUTCOME (Conditional)

  • Icon: shield (filled)
  • Content: What the DLP system did — three possible states:
  • Note: "DLP alert is an enforcement fact, not a resolution"
  • DLP system tags show which system was involved (e.g., Purview, Netskope)
AXIA
9.5 Source Content Preview

9.5 Source Content Preview

Expandable collapsible section:

ElementDescription
HeaderSource type icon (email/file), "Source Content", timestamp
Email MetadataFrom, To, CC, Subject fields
Content PreviewTruncated message body with option to expand
Attachment CardsFilename, file type icon, size, DLP status badge
Sensitive HighlightingAI-identified sensitive portions highlighted in the preview (P2)
AXIA
9.6 Document Viewer

9.6 Document Viewer

Accessed by double-clicking an attachment in Source Content Preview:

FeatureDescription
Document DisplayRendered view of the attachment content
Sensitivity HighlightingYellow/orange highlights on sensitive sections identified by AI
Highlight LegendCategories of highlighted content (PII, Financial, IP, Confidential)
NavigationBack to Case Details, zoom controls
InteractionHover over highlight shows tooltip explaining why section is sensitive
AXIA
9.7 Possible Next Actions (Ope...

9.7 Possible Next Actions (Open Cases Only)

Checkbox list organized by action category:

Engagement Actions

  • ☐ Coach employee for context (Recommended for Medium severity)
  • ☐ Notify manager for review (Recommended for High severity)
  • ☐ Notify Legal and Security leadership (Recommended for Critical severity)
  • ☐ Notify HR for employment context

Investigation Actions

  • ☐ Preserve evidence for investigation
  • ☐ Expand scope to related cases
  • ☐ Request additional context from Intel
  • ☐ Escalate for forensic analysis
  • Confirm Selected Actions (N) — Primary button with count of selected actions
  • View Generated Drafts — Secondary button for pre-written communications
  • Ask Intel About This Case — Navigates to Intel with case context loaded
  • Actions marked "Recommended" are pre-checked based on severity playbook
  • Recommended items have teal border/background for visual distinction
  • User can override any pre-selection
AXIA
9.8 Activity Timeline

9.8 Activity Timeline

Immutable chronological log:

ElementDescription
Vertical LineConnecting timeline entries
Colored DotsEvent-type-specific colors (primary=AXIA actions, teal=engagement, severity=risk, red=blocked, gray=informational)
TimestampExact time of event
Event DescriptionWhat happened (e.g., "AXIA flagged as Critical based on behavioral analysis")
SourceSystem or person that triggered (e.g., "AXIA AI Engine", "Analyst: Sarah Kim")

Rules:

  • All entries are immutable (no delete/edit)
  • Actions confirmed by user create new timeline entries
  • DLP events, AXIA analysis, human actions all appear in unified timeline

Screenshot: Case Details — Actions & Timeline — Engagement actions (Contact Employee/Manager/Legal/HR), enforcement options (Create DLP Rule, Activate Smart Block), and immutable activity timeline showing the full case chronology.

AXIA
10. Insights Tab — Analytics D...

10. Insights Tab — Analytics Dashboard

Phase: P2 — This screen is not included in P1 scope. Requires accumulated interaction data for meaningful analytics. Specifications below define the target state for P2 delivery.
AXIA
10.1 Intent

10.1 Intent

Provide executive-level analytics that tell the AXIA value story — demonstrating platform effectiveness, false positive reduction, time savings, and risk posture improvement over time.

Screenshot: Insights Dashboard — Fixed 8-widget analytics layout: Overall Posture (RCS + MTTR), AXIA Actions breakdown, AXIA Impact (before/after KPIs), Cases timeline, Top Case Types, Departments heatmap, Alerts vs. True Incidents, and True Incidents trend.

AXIA
10.2 Header

10.2 Header

ElementDescription
Screen Title"Insights"
Subtitle"Performance & Value Metrics"
Time Selector1d / 7d / 30d / 90d (syncs all widgets)
AXIA
10.3 Dashboard Widgets

10.3 Dashboard Widgets

The Insights dashboard uses a fixed, purposeful layout — not customizable, following the Wiz model. Widgets are organized by value story.

Figure: Insights Dashboard Layout — Fixed 8-widget dashboard grid showing spatial arrangement and visual hierarchy of the analytics dashboard.

Widget 1: Risk Control Score

  • Type: Area chart with prominent current score
  • Data: Composite score (0-100) tracking organizational risk posture
  • Composition: Weighted average of Cases Closed Rate (40%), False Positive Reduction (30%), MTTR Performance (30%)
  • Time Story: Score improves from ~58 (at 90d baseline) to ~94 (at 1d current)
  • Visual: Area fill under score line, real date labels (e.g., Nov, Dec, Jan)
  • Interaction: Hover shows score breakdown

Widget 2: Mean Time to Resolution (MTTR)

  • Type: Sparkline chart with prominent current value
  • Data: Average hours from case open to resolution
  • Hero Metric: Current MTTR (e.g., "0.5h")
  • Time Story: 90d view shows dramatic improvement (e.g., 48h baseline to 0.5h current)
  • Visual: Trend line with big value display

Widget 3: Cases by Severity

  • Type: Donut chart with center total
  • Data: Distribution across Critical/High/Medium/Low
  • Segments: Critical (red), High (orange), Medium (blue), Low (gray)
  • Center: Total case count
  • Interaction: Click segment navigates to Command with severity filter applied

Widget 4: Cases by Status

  • Type: Segmented progress bar
  • Data: Open / In Progress / Closed breakdown
  • Visual: Horizontal stacked bar with color coding

Widget 5: AXIA Actions

  • Type: Vertical stacked bar chart
  • Data: Actions by type over time
  • Categories: Coach Employee, Escalate to Manager, Notify Legal, Notify HR
  • Time Story: Shows volume and mix of actions across time periods
  • Visual: Stacked bars with category colors

Widget 6: Signal vs. Noise

  • Type: Dual-area chart
  • Data: Total DLP alerts (high line) vs. AXIA verified true alerts (lower line)
  • Key Metric: Noise reduction percentage (gap between lines)
  • Time Story: 90d shows DLP generating many alerts while AXIA filters to true signal
  • Visual: Gray area for DLP noise, primary color for true signal, annotation showing noise reduction %

Widget 7: Department Risk Heatmap

  • Type: Grid heatmap (departments as rows, severity as columns)
  • Data: Case count by department and severity
  • Departments: Engineering, Sales, Finance, HR, Legal, Operations, Product, Executive
  • Columns: Critical, High, Medium, Low
  • Visual: Color intensity represents case count; hover shows exact count

Widget 8: Top Case Types (V6.21 New)

  • Type: Ranked list with progress bars
  • Data: 6 most common case types with frequency counts
  • Visual: Type label + count + horizontal bar showing relative frequency
  • Examples: "Pre-departure exfiltration", "Unauthorized external sharing", "Policy bypass", "Sensitive data in wrong channel", "Bulk data export", "Encrypted content bypass"
AXIA
10.4 Value Story by Time Range

10.4 Value Story by Time Range

Time RangeStoryKey Data
1d"Current operational excellence"Today's performance; all metrics should show strong state
7d"This week's trends"Stability of improvements; minor fluctuations acceptable
30d"This month's progress"Improvement trajectory; validates AXIA's ongoing value
90d"The AXIA transformation"Before/after comparison; dramatic improvement; shows ROI. This is the view for CISOs and boards.
AXIA
10.5 Design Rules

10.5 Design Rules

  • Fixed layout — No drag-and-drop or widget customization
  • Purposeful — Every widget tells part of the AXIA value story
  • Real dates — Chart labels use actual month/day names, not generic "M1/M2"
  • Time selector syncs all widgets — Changing time range updates everything
  • No vanity metrics — Every number connects to business value
AXIA
11. Intel Tab — Conversational...

11. Intel Tab — Conversational AI Advisor

11.1 Intent

A conversational AI interface for strategic risk analysis, pattern discovery, and document assessment. Intel is AXIA's "brain" made accessible through natural language.

AXIA
11.2 Welcome State (Empty Chat...

11.2 Welcome State (Empty Chat)

ElementDescription
IconAXIA brand icon
Headline"How can I help?"
Subhead"Ask me about risk patterns, analyze documents, or get strategic recommendations."
Quick Prompt ChipsHorizontal scrollable buttons with suggested queries

Default Quick Prompt Chips:

  • "Top risk users this week"
  • "Department risk breakdown"
  • "Show departing employee activity"
  • "Unusual access patterns"
  • "Incident trends this month"
  • "Compare DLP vs. AXIA detection"

Screenshot: Intel Tab — Welcome State — Conversational AI advisor with quick-action chips for common risk queries, input field with file attachment support, and suggested prompts.

AXIA
11.3 Chat Interface

11.3 Chat Interface

Screenshot: Intel Tab — AI Conversation — Live investigation showing AXIA's structured response to a risk query: behavioral anomaly analysis, file access patterns, timeline of actions taken, and recommended escalation path.

User Messages

  • Right-aligned bubble, primary color background
  • File attachment preview if attached
  • Timestamp: "You - 10:30 AM"

AI Responses

  • Left-aligned card-style bubble
  • "AXIA Intelligence" label with avatar
  • Markdown rendering support (tables, lists, code blocks, emphasis)
  • Clickable action buttons within responses (see 11.5)
  • Timestamp

Typing Indicator

  • "Thinking..." status with animated dots
AXIA
11.4 Document Assessment Capab...

11.4 Document Assessment Capability

Intel supports document upload for risk analysis:

FeatureSpecification
File Attachment ButtonOpens file picker
Max File Size20MB
Supported FormatsPDF, DOC, DOCX, TXT, CSV, XLSX, XLS, PPT, PPTX, MD, JSON
Context FieldsWhen uploading, user can optionally specify: Sender, Recipient, Department
Analysis OutputSensitivity classification, content categories detected, specific findings, risk indicators, recommended handling

Document Assessment Flow:

  1. User clicks attachment button, selects file
  2. File preview shows with optional context fields (Sender, Recipient)
  3. User sends with optional message (e.g., "Assess this document for risk")
  4. AI responds with structured risk assessment
AXIA
11.5 Actionable Recommendation...

11.5 Actionable Recommendations

When Intel provides recommendations, they include action buttons:

Button TypeDestination
[View Cases ->]Navigate to Command with filter applied
[View Case ->]Navigate to specific Case Details
[Draft Communication ->]Open engagement draft modal
[View Policy ->]Open uploaded policy document in Settings
[See Similar Cases ->]Navigate to Command with search query
AXIA
11.6 Case Context Mode

11.6 Case Context Mode

When Intel is invoked from Case Details:

  • Header shows: "Discussing: CASE-XXXX"
  • AI has access to: Full case details, entity profile, related cases, case timeline
  • Suggested prompts change to case-specific questions:

Exiting Case Context:

  • "New Conversation" button clears case context
  • Or user asks unrelated question (Intel asks to confirm context switch)
AXIA
11.7 Pattern Analysis Cards

11.7 Pattern Analysis Cards

For certain queries, Intel generates visual analysis cards alongside text:

Card TypeContent
Entity ProfileName, role, department, risk score, case count, behavioral summary
Trend ChartMini sparkline showing relevant metric over time
Comparison TableSide-by-side comparison (e.g., user vs. peer baseline)
Risk DistributionMini severity breakdown for the queried scope
AXIA
11.8 Input Area

11.8 Input Area

Fixed at bottom of screen:

ElementDescription
File Attachment ButtonOpens file picker
Text InputAuto-expanding textarea, placeholder: "Ask AXIA anything..."
Send ButtonPrimary-colored, arrow icon
Quick Prompt ChipsContext-sensitive, scrollable horizontal chips
AXIA
12. Enforce Tab — DLP Integrat...

12. Enforce Tab — DLP Integration & Monitoring

Phase: P3 — This screen is not included in P1 or P2 scope. Requires organizational intelligence from P2 behavioral baselines plus enforcement loop architecture. Specifications below define the target state for P3 delivery.
AXIA
12.1 Intent

12.1 Intent

The Enforce tab shows AXIA's integration with DLP enforcement systems, providing visibility into connected DLP system health, event ingestion, and correlation status.

Screenshot: Enforce Tab — DLP system connections (Microsoft Purview, Netskope) with coverage and sync status, available integrations (Symantec, Forcepoint, Digital Guardian, etc.), and Smart Block contextual enforcement policies.

Screenshot: Enforce — Smart Block Policies — Contextual enforcement rules using behavioral signals that static DLP rules cannot capture: pre-departure data collection, financial data blackout, CRM export alerts, competitor communication monitoring, after-hours access, and personal cloud upload detection.

AXIA
12.2 DLP Systems Section

12.2 DLP Systems Section

Displays connected DLP systems with status:

Connected DLP Systems

FieldDescription
System Namee.g., "Netskope"
Scopee.g., "Non-Microsoft Environment" (for Netskope), "Microsoft Environment" (for Purview)
CoveragePercentage of monitored assets covered
Integration StatusActive/Degraded/Disconnected with color indicator
Sync StatusReal-time / Batch with last sync time
StatsEvents ingested, alerts correlated

Primary DLP Integration

DLP SystemScopePriorityIntegration Type
NetskopeNon-Microsoft (SaaS, cloud storage, web, CASB)P1REST API, bi-directional

Additional DLP Systems (Based on Customer Demand)

Displayed as cards with "Available" or "Coming Soon" status:

SystemStatus
Microsoft PurviewAvailable
Symantec DLPAvailable
Forcepoint DLPAvailable
Digital GuardianAvailable
Trellix DLPComing Soon
Proofpoint DLPComing Soon
Code42 IncydrComing Soon
Zscaler Data ProtectionComing Soon
Palo Alto Enterprise DLPComing Soon
AXIA
13. Posture Tab — Risk Posture...

13. Posture Tab — Risk Posture Overview

Phase: P2 — This screen is not included in P1 scope. Requires resolved-case history and trend data from P1 operations. Specifications below define the target state for P2 delivery.
AXIA
13.1 Intent

13.1 Intent

Provide an at-a-glance view of the organization's overall insider risk posture with severity distribution, posture score, and key health indicators.

Screenshot: Posture Tab — Per-source security overview showing interaction volume, case counts, engagement breakdown, and DLP alert statistics for each connected data source (Office 365, Gmail, Slack).

AXIA
13.2 Components

13.2 Components

ComponentDescription
Overall Posture ScoreComposite score (0-100) with grade (A-F) and trend arrow
Severity DistributionVisual breakdown of active cases by severity with counts
Posture MetricsKey health indicators: Open case count, avg resolution time, coverage %, engagement rate
Department OverviewSummary of risk by department with severity indicators
Trend ComparisonCurrent posture vs. 30 days ago vs. 90 days ago
AXIA
13.3 Posture Score Composition

13.3 Posture Score Composition

The Posture Score is derived from the same composite as the Risk Control Score in Insights but optimized for snapshot viewing:

FactorWeightDescription
Cases Resolved Rate35%% of cases resolved within SLA
False Positive Reduction30%Improvement in FP rate since deployment
MTTR20%Mean time to resolution vs. target
Coverage15%% of data channels connected and healthy
AXIA
14. Connect Tab — Data Source ...

14. Connect Tab — Data Source Management

14.1 Intent

Manage all data source connections. The Connect tab shows what AXIA is monitoring, what's available in the environment, and what's planned for future support.

Screenshot: Connect Tab — Data source management showing connected email channels (M365, Google Workspace), SaaS channels (Slack), and context sources (Okta Identity, Workday HR) with sync status and interaction counts.

AXIA
14.2 Connection States

14.2 Connection States

StateVisualDescription
ConnectedSolid green badge, green checkmarkActive, data flowing
AvailableGreen outline badgeAuto-detected in customer environment, ready to connect
Coming SoonGray badgeNot yet supported, on roadmap
ErrorRed badgeConnection issue, needs attention
AXIA
14.3 Data Source Categories

14.3 Data Source Categories

Email Channels

SourcePriorityIntegration Type
Microsoft 365 EmailP1Graph API
Google GmailP1Gmail API

SaaS Channels

SourcePriorityIntegration Type
Microsoft TeamsP1Graph API
Slack EnterpriseP1Events API
Microsoft SharePointP2Graph API
Google DriveP2Drive API

Identity & Organizational Context Sources

SourcePriorityIntegration Type
Excel / CSV / XLSX UploadP1Manual file import with column mapping
Word / Document UploadP1Org chart and hierarchy import
Microsoft Entra IDP2SCIM + OAuth 2.0
OktaP2SCIM + OAuth 2.0
WorkdayP2REST API
SAP SuccessFactorsP2OData API
ADPP2REST API
Google WorkspaceP2Directory API + OAuth
BambooHRP3REST API
Ping IdentityP3SCIM + SAML
On-premises ADP3LDAP sync agent

DLP Systems (also shown in Enforce tab)

SourcePriorityIntegration Type
NetskopeP1REST API
Microsoft PurviewBased on demandGraph/Compliance API
Symantec DLPBased on demandAPI + Syslog
Forcepoint DLPBased on demandAPI
AXIA
14.4 Connection Flow

14.4 Connection Flow

Interactive modal for connecting a new data source:

  1. Select Source — Click an "Available" card
  2. Authentication — OAuth flow or API key entry
  3. Permission Review — Show what data AXIA will access, request consent
  4. Test Connection — Verify connectivity, show success/failure
  5. Initial Sync — Progress indicator for first data ingestion
  6. Complete — Source appears as "Connected" with green status

Figure: Connection Flow Wizard — Six-step guided connection process with progress tracking and error handling.

AXIA
14.5 Connected Source Details ...

14.5 Connected Source Details (Expanded View)

When clicking a connected source:

FieldDescription
Connection StatusConnected since [date], last sync [time], health indicator
Coverage StatsMessages indexed, cases generated, DLP alerts correlated
Data RangeEarliest and most recent data
Connection DetailsAPI endpoint, tenant ID (masked), permissions granted
ActionsTest Connection, Reconfigure, Disconnect
AXIA
15. Settings Tab — Administrat...

15. Settings Tab — Administration & Configuration

15.1 Intent

Screenshot: Settings — Organizational Guidance — Document upload interface for organizational policies (Acceptable Use Policy, Data Classification Guide) that help AXIA understand specific risk tolerance and business context.

Configure AXIA's behavior, AI boundaries, organizational context, and user management. All options are visible and discoverable — no hidden AI behavior.

AXIA
15.2 Settings Sections

15.2 Settings Sections

15.2.1 Case Action Options

Toggle controls for available case actions:

CategoryOptions
Employee ActionsCoach Employee (toggle), Notify Employee (toggle)
Manager ActionsNotify Manager (toggle), Escalate to Manager (toggle)
Legal ActionsNotify Legal (toggle), Preserve Evidence (toggle)
HR ActionsNotify HR (toggle), Request Employment Context (toggle)

Each toggle can be enabled/disabled globally. Disabled actions do not appear in Case Details.

Screenshot: Settings — Case Action Options — Toggle controls for all case actions (Contact Employee, Manager, Legal, HR; Create/Adjust DLP Rules; Activate Smart Block) with Manual/Auto mode selection per action.

15.2.2 Organizational Guidance

Upload area for context documents that AXIA uses to calibrate analysis:

Document TypePurposeFormat
Acceptable Use PolicyDefines what's permitted for data handlingPDF, DOCX, TXT
Data Classification GuideSensitivity levels and handling rulesPDF, DOCX, TXT
Incident Response PlaybookEscalation procedures and decision treesPDF, DOCX
Engagement TemplatesTone/style guidelines for communicationsDOCX, TXT
Domain Reputation ListKnown-safe and known-risky external domainsCSV

Upload UX:

  • Dashed border drop zone with file picker
  • Uploaded files show in list with filename, upload date, file size
  • Status indicator (Processing / Active / Error)
  • Delete with confirmation prompt
  • Max 10MB per file

15.2.3 AI Boundaries (Critical for Trust)

Two-section display with clear visual distinction. Expanded by default.

What AXIA May Suggest (Green indicators):

  • Preserve evidence for investigation
  • Notify manager or stakeholders
  • Generate draft communications
  • Escalate to Legal or Security
  • Recommend enforcement policy changes
  • Contact employees directly without human approval
  • Modify, delete, or block any data or communications
  • Share case details with external parties
  • Make disciplinary recommendations
  • Change security policies without human confirmation
  • Access data outside the authorized scope

15.2.4 User Management

FeatureDescription
User ListName, email, role, last active, status
Role AssignmentSecurity Admin, Analyst, Legal, HR/Manager, Executive
SSO ConfigurationSAML 2.0 / OIDC integration settings
MFA EnforcementRequired for all users (non-negotiable)

15.2.5 Engagement Automation (P1: Display Only, P2: Active)

Controls for automated engagement behavior. In P1, these are visible but disabled to show the platform's automation roadmap.

ToggleDescriptionP1 StateP2 State
Auto-Coach (Low)Automatically send coaching emails for Low severity casesGreyed out — "Available in P2"Active toggle (on/off)
Auto-Coach (Medium)Automatically send coaching emails for Medium severity casesGreyed out — "Available in P2"Active toggle (on/off)
Auto-Notify Manager (High)Automatically notify manager for High severity casesGreyed out — "Available in P2"Active toggle (on/off, with approval workflow option)
Auto-Preserve EvidenceAutomatically preserve evidence for High/Critical casesGreyed out — "Available in P2"Active toggle (on/off)

UX:

  • Section header: "Engagement Automation" with "(Coming in P2)" badge in P1
  • Each toggle row shows the control (greyed), label, and info icon
  • Info icon opens popover explaining what the automation will do and when it becomes available
  • In P2, badge changes to "Active" and toggles become functional

15.2.6 Notification Preferences

ChannelOptions
EmailCase created (by severity), case assigned, action required
In-AppAll events, with sound/badge options
SIEM/SOARWebhook configuration for external alerting

15.2.7 Display Preferences

SettingOptions
ThemeLight (Default) / Dark / System (match OS)
Date FormatMM/DD/YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD
Time ZoneAuto-detect or manual selection
LanguageEnglish (additional languages on roadmap)
AXIA
16. AI/ML Engine Requirements

16. AI/ML Engine Requirements

This section defines what the AI does (functional requirements), not how it's built. Capabilities are phased; see Section 4 for phase definitions. Latency targets in this section are superseded by Section 4.5 AI Response Time Requirements.
AXIA
16.1 Case Risk Scoring (P1)

16.1 Case Risk Scoring (P1)

AspectSpecification
Inputs (P1)DLP alert payload, source content, entity context (role/dept/tenure/status from directory/HR), organizational policies, recipient context
Inputs (P2+)All P1 inputs + behavioral baseline, individual pattern history, peer-group baselines
OutputsRisk Score (0-100), Severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low), Confidence Level (High/Med/Low), Contributing Factors list
Success Criteria>90% severity agreement with expert reviewers, <3% FP rate for Critical/High, <1% FN rate
Latency< 500ms from DLP alert receipt to risk score
AXIA
16.2 Structured Case Analysis ...

16.2 Structured Case Analysis Generation (P1)

AspectSpecification
InputsCase data, entity profile, behavioral baseline, DLP alert details, organizational policies
OutputsWhat Happened (factual), Why It Matters (impact), Why It Is Unusual (deviation), DLP Outcome (conditional)
Success CriteriaFactually accurate (no hallucination), non-technical language, executive-readable
Latency< 3 seconds for complete analysis
AXIA
16.3 Intel Conversational Quer...

16.3 Intel Conversational Queries (P1)

AspectSpecification
InputsUser query (up to 4,000 chars), uploaded documents, case database, entity database, organizational context, conversation history
OutputsMarkdown-formatted response, source citations, suggested follow-ups, confidence indicators, actionable buttons
Query TypesEntity profiles, risk patterns, flight risk analysis, policy questions, document analysis, scenario simulation
Success Criteria85% relevant results, grounded in actual data (no fabrication), actionable
Latency< 5 seconds conversational, < 30 seconds document analysis
AXIA
16.4 Behavioral Anomaly Detect...

16.4 Behavioral Anomaly Detection (P2)

AspectSpecification
InputsEmployee communication patterns (volume, recipients, timing, channels), role-based baseline, individual baseline, HR signals, 30-90 day time series
OutputsAnomaly flag, anomaly type, deviation magnitude, context narrative, recommended action
Success Criteria>85% precision, <5% FP rate, correlation with actual risk events
Latency< 5 minutes for daily baseline recalculation per entity
AXIA
16.5 Engagement Content Genera...

16.5 Engagement Content Generation (P1: Recommendation Only, P2: Auto-Send)

AspectSpecification
InputsCase context, engagement type, organizational tone/style, severity playbook, allowed actions from Settings
OutputsDraft message (email/chat-ready), recipient, tone classification, recommended action rationale (linked to What/Why/Unusual analysis), subject line
P1 BehaviorGenerates draft recommendations for all five engagement types. All outputs require manual operator approval. Drafts are presented in Case Details via "View Generated Drafts" button.
P2 BehaviorSame generation capability, but automation toggles in Settings allow auto-send for configured severity levels without manual approval.
Success Criteria80% pass human review without substantive edits, appropriate tone, no accusatory language
Latency< 10 seconds for draft generation (see Section 4.5)
AXIA
16.6 Document Sensitivity Anal...

16.6 Document Sensitivity Analysis (P1)

AspectSpecification
InputsDocument content (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, etc.), metadata, classification rules, analysis context
OutputsSensitivity classification (Public/Internal/Confidential/Highly Restricted), content categories (PII, financial, IP, legal, HR), specific findings with positions, risk indicators, recommended handling
Success Criteria>95% precision for category detection, no PII false negatives
Latency< 30 seconds for documents up to 20MB
AXIA
16.7 Interactive AI Capabiliti...

16.7 Interactive AI Capabilities (P1: Limited, P2: Full)

Intel supports three modes of user interaction that define how analysts and leaders work with AXIA's AI:

ModeDescriptionExamples
Information & DiscussionGet information from the system and discuss it conversationally. Users ask questions, explore data, and have back-and-forth dialogue with AXIA about risk patterns, entity behavior, and organizational context."Show me departing employees with high-risk activity", "What's the trend in engineering data sharing?", "Compare this quarter's risk to last quarter"
Review & AssessmentReview material and context for security assessment. Users upload documents, policies, or case evidence for AI-driven analysis. AXIA provides structured assessments with sensitivity classifications, risk indicators, and policy alignment checks."Assess this document for sensitive content", "Review this policy against our classification guide", "What risk does this case evidence present?"
System OrchestrationOrchestrate system actions through conversational commands. Users direct AXIA to perform actions: draft communications, create case actions, connect to new data sources, or adjust system configuration."Draft a coaching email for this employee", "Send a manager notification about this case", "Add this domain to our reputation list"

These three modes enable Intel to serve as both an analytical tool and a command interface, making AXIA's full capability accessible through natural language.

AXIA
16.8 Explainability Requiremen...

16.8 Explainability Requirements (P1)

RequirementDescription
Structured ReasoningEvery risk score accompanied by What/Why/Why Unusual in plain language
Factor AttributionEach explanation cites specific factors (e.g., "recipient is competitor domain")
Confidence IndicatorsHigh/Medium/Low with supporting rationale
Audit TrailAll AI decisions logged with inputs, model version, outputs for post-hoc review
No Black BoxesAnalysts can understand conclusions without ML knowledge
Source GroundingIntel responses reference actual data — no fabrication
AXIA
16.9 Organizational Customizat...

16.9 Organizational Customization (P1)

TypeHow It Works
Policy DocumentsCustomer uploads AUP, classification guides, playbooks. AI references in analysis.
Severity PlaybooksCustomer defines recommended actions per severity. AI pre-selects accordingly.
Engagement TemplatesCustomer provides tone/style guidelines. AI generates matching drafts.
Role BaselinesAI learns normal patterns per role/department from communication analysis. No manual config.
Domain ReputationCustomer flags known-safe or known-risky external domains. AI incorporates into scoring.
Feedback LoopAnalyst actions (severity adjustments, resolutions) inform future scoring without explicit training.
AXIA
16.10 Safety Guardrails (P1)

16.10 Safety Guardrails (P1)

GuardrailImplementation
Action BoundariesAI may only suggest; execution requires human confirmation (system-enforced)
Escalation TriggersCritical cases always surface for human review; no silent handling
Content FilteringAI responses filtered for inappropriate content, bias, discriminatory language
Rate LimitingMaximum engagement frequency per employee to prevent harassment
Adversarial RobustnessResistance to prompt injection via uploaded documents or email content
Fail-Safe DefaultsOn AI error or uncertainty, escalate to human rather than suppress
Privacy PreservationCustomer data never used to train models shared with other customers
AXIA
17. Engagement & Response Syst...

17. Engagement & Response System

17.1 Engagement Philosophy

AXIA's engagement system is a key differentiator. Unlike DLP (which blocks) or IRM (which alerts), AXIA orchestrates conversations and tracks resolution.

The engagement system evolves across phases:

  • P1: AI generates all engagement drafts and recommendations. Operator reviews, edits, and manually approves before any communication is sent.
  • P2: Configurable automation. System can auto-send for configured severity levels based on Settings toggles. Legal/Critical escalations always require human confirmation.
  • P3: Full orchestration. System manages email threads, follows up on unresolved outreach, and auto-closes cases based on response patterns.

Figure: Engagement State Machine — Lifecycle states from Draft Generated to Resolved, showing P1 (manual), P2 (configurable auto-send), and P3 (full orchestration) paths.

AXIA
17.2 Engagement Types

17.2 Engagement Types

TypeTriggerToneRecipientsSeverity
Employee CoachingLow/Medium severity, first occurrenceSupportive, educationalEmployeeLow, Medium
Manager NotificationHigh severity, pattern detectedFactual, action-orientedDirect managerHigh
Legal EscalationCritical severity, regulatory riskFormal, evidence-focusedLegal counsel, CISOCritical
HR NotificationEmployment context neededNeutral, information-seekingHR business partnerMedium, High
Evidence PreservationAny severity with legal exposureProceduralLegal/ComplianceHigh, Critical
AXIA
17.3 Draft Communications (P1:...

17.3 Draft Communications (P1: Generated for approval, P2+: Can auto-send)

For each engagement type, AXIA generates context-appropriate draft communications:

Draft TypeContent
Employee Coaching EmailReference to event (without accusation), explanation of policy, request for context, guidance on correct procedure
Manager BriefingCase summary, What/Why/Unusual, recommended actions, link to case in AXIA
Legal EscalationFormal incident summary, evidence catalog, regulatory exposure assessment, recommended legal hold actions
HR Context RequestNeutral inquiry about employment status, performance context, upcoming changes
AXIA
17.4 Engagement Tracking

17.4 Engagement Tracking

All engagements are tracked in the Case Activity Timeline:

Tracked FieldDescription
Sent timestampWhen the communication was sent
RecipientWho received it
TypeCoaching/Notification/Escalation
Response receivedWhether the recipient responded
ResolutionWhat happened as a result
Time to responseDuration from send to response
AXIA
17.5 Severity-Based Playbooks

17.5 Severity-Based Playbooks

SeverityDefault Actions (Pre-checked)P1 BehaviorP2 Behavior
CriticalPreserve evidence, Notify Legal, Escalate to CISOAI drafts all; operator must approve eachAI drafts all; operator must approve each (always human-confirmed)
HighNotify manager, Preserve evidenceAI drafts all; operator must approve eachAuto-notify manager if toggle enabled in Settings; evidence auto-preserved
MediumCoach employeeAI drafts coaching email; operator must approveAuto-coach if toggle enabled in Settings
LowAuto-resolve if matches known-good patternAI recommends resolution; operator confirmsAuto-resolve enabled by default for verified false positives

Figure: Severity Playbook Matrix — Visual decision grid mapping severity levels to default actions with P1 (Manual) vs P2 (Auto) behavior badges.

AXIA
18. Non-Functional Requirement...

18. Non-Functional Requirements

18.1 Performance

Note: AI processing latency targets (risk scoring, analysis generation, Intel response, document analysis) are defined in Section 4.5 AI Response Time Requirements and supersede the values below. The targets below apply to UI rendering and non-AI operations.
MetricTarget
Page Load Time< 2 seconds (all screens)
Case List Render (Command)< 2 seconds for 100 cases
Case List Render (Cases tab)< 2 seconds for 500 cases with active filters
Case Details Load< 3 seconds (UI rendering; AI analysis generation per Section 4.5)
Intel ResponsePer Section 4.5: < 10 seconds (conversation), < 60 seconds (document analysis)
Risk ScoringPer Section 4.5: < 6 seconds from event receipt to risk score
Dashboard Refresh< 3 seconds (all widgets) — P2
Search Results< 3 seconds
AXIA
18.2 Security

18.2 Security

RequirementSpecification
AuthenticationSAML 2.0 and OIDC SSO; MFA enforced for all users
AuthorizationRole-based access control (RBAC) mapped from IdP groups
Encryption at RestAES-256 for all stored data
Encryption in TransitTLS 1.3 for all communications
Key ManagementCustomer-managed encryption keys (BYOK) for enterprise tier
Audit LoggingAll actions logged immutably with timestamp, actor, context
Session ManagementConfigurable session timeout, concurrent session limits
API SecurityOAuth 2.0 + API Key, rate limiting (1,000 req/min), signed webhooks (HMAC)
AXIA
18.3 Privacy

18.3 Privacy

RequirementImplementation
Data MinimizationCollect only data necessary for risk assessment
Purpose LimitationData used only for insider risk analysis as authorized
No Employee ScoringNo ranking, gamification, or surveillance-style profiling
Metadata-Only ModeAvailable for privacy-sensitive customers (no content analysis)
Data RetentionAligned with customer policies; configurable retention periods
Right of AccessSupport data subject access requests (DSAR)
ConsentContent access requires explicit legal authorization and user consent notice
Cross-Tenant IsolationNo data sharing across tenants; AI models not trained on customer data
AXIA
18.4 Accessibility

18.4 Accessibility

RequirementStandard
WCAG LevelAA compliance (4.5:1 contrast minimum)
Touch Targets44x44px minimum for all interactive elements
Focus IndicatorsVisible 2px primary-color outline
Color IndependenceNever use color alone to convey information; always pair with icon/text
Screen ReaderAll interactive elements have ARIA labels
Keyboard NavigationFull application navigable via keyboard (Tab, Enter, Escape)
Responsive DesignFunctional on 1024px+ viewports; degraded-but-usable on tablets
AXIA
18.5 Compliance

18.5 Compliance

StandardStatusNotes
SOC 2 Type IIRequiredSecurity, Availability, Confidentiality
ISO 27001RequiredInformation Security Management
GDPRRequiredEU data subject rights, data minimization
CCPARequiredCalifornia consumer privacy rights
HIPAARoadmapFor healthcare customers (BAA required)
FedRAMPRoadmapFor US federal government customers
AXIA
18.6 Availability & Reliabilit...

18.6 Availability & Reliability

MetricTarget
Uptime SLA99.9% (excludes scheduled maintenance)
Planned Maintenance< 4 hours/month, off-peak hours with advance notice
RTO< 4 hours
RPO< 1 hour
FailoverAutomatic multi-AZ deployment with health checks
AXIA
19. Integration Requirements

19. Integration Requirements

Figure: Integration Ecosystem Map — Hub-and-spoke diagram showing AXIA's integration categories with phase priorities and data flow directionality.

AXIA
19.1 DLP System Integrations

19.1 DLP System Integrations

DLP SystemPriorityIntegration TypeData Flow
NetskopeP1REST APIBi-directional: Ingest events + provide intelligence
Microsoft PurviewBased on demandGraph / Compliance APIIngest alerts
Symantec DLPBased on demandAPI + SyslogIngest incidents, policy violations
Forcepoint DLPBased on demandAPIIngest policy events, classifications
Digital GuardianBased on demandAPIIngest endpoint events, data movement
Proofpoint DLPBased on demandAPIIngest email DLP events
ZscalerBased on demandAPIIngest cloud security events
Generic SIEM/SyslogBased on demandSyslog/CEFAny DLP that exports to SIEM format

Key Requirements:

  • Near real-time ingestion (< 60 seconds from DLP event to AXIA case creation)
  • Historical import for pilot/deployment (last 30-90 days)
AXIA
19.2 Identity & Organizational...

19.2 Identity & Organizational Context Integrations

Identity providers and HR systems serve the same purpose: understanding who people are, their roles, reporting structure, and organizational context. They are merged into a single integration category with a phased approach starting from manual import.

P1 — Manual Import (Day-One Capability)

MethodPriorityData Imported
Excel / CSV / XLSX UploadP1Employee roster: name, email, role, department, manager, employment status, cost center
Word / Structured Document UploadP1Org charts, reporting hierarchies, department structures

Manual import enables immediate deployment without requiring API integrations. Customers upload their employee directory and org structure from any source (HR system export, IT directory export, manually maintained roster).

P2 — Identity Provider & HR System API Integrations

Provider / SystemPriorityIntegration TypeCritical Data Points
Microsoft Entra IDP2SCIM + OAuth 2.0User profiles, group membership, role assignments
OktaP2SCIM + OAuth 2.0User profiles, group membership, role assignments
WorkdayP2REST APIResignation/termination date, manager hierarchy, department, cost center
SAP SuccessFactorsP2OData APIEmployment status, org structure
ADPP2REST APIEmployment status, termination alerts
Google WorkspaceP2Directory API + OAuthUser profiles, org units
Generic HRISP2CSV/SFTP scheduled importAutomated recurring import from HR system exports

P3 — Extended Identity Providers

ProviderPriorityIntegration Type
Ping IdentityP3SCIM + SAML
On-premises ADP3LDAP sync agent
BambooHRP3REST API

Key Requirements:

  • P1 manual import must support drag-and-drop file upload with column mapping UI
  • P1 import validates data completeness (warns on missing manager, department, or role fields)
  • P2 API integrations provide automated sync (configurable frequency: hourly, daily, weekly)
  • System reconciles manual imports with API data when both are present (API takes precedence, manual fills gaps)
AXIA
19.3 Communication Platform In...

19.3 Communication Platform Integrations

Engineering Status Note (Feb 2026): The team is well ahead of original PRD phasing on communication connectors. Outlook connector is implemented (MVP-324, MVP-329), Teams connector is in progress (MVP-343, MVP-344), Slack research is complete (MVP-299) with implementation queued (MVP-322). Gmail API migration is done (MVP-274). Priorities below reflect updated phasing based on engineering progress.
PlatformPriorityData IngestedEngineering Status
Google GmailP1Email metadata, content (with consent), attachmentsDone (MVP-274: migrated to Gmail API)
Microsoft 365 Email (Outlook)P1Email metadata, content (with consent), attachments, OneDrive linksDone (MVP-324, MVP-328, MVP-329)
Microsoft TeamsP1Chat messages, file sharing (with consent)In Progress (MVP-343, MVP-344)
Slack EnterpriseP1Messages, file sharing, channel activityResearch done (MVP-299), implementation queued (MVP-322)
Microsoft SharePointP2Document sharing, access events
Google DriveP2File sharing, access eventsResearch started (MVP-326: Drive folder paths)
AXIA
19.4 SIEM/SOAR Integrations

19.4 SIEM/SOAR Integrations

PlatformPriorityUse Case
SplunkP3Send AXIA alerts; ingest related events
Microsoft SentinelP3Alert sync
IBM QRadarP3Alert forwarding
Palo Alto XSOARP3Automated playbook triggers
ServiceNow SecOpsP3Incident ticketing, workflow
AXIA
19.5 REST API for Custom Integ...

19.5 REST API for Custom Integrations

EndpointDescription
Cases APICRUD operations, search, filter
Entities APIRead employee profiles, risk scores
Intel APISubmit queries, retrieve responses
WebhooksPush notifications for case events (HMAC-signed)
Bulk ExportScheduled data exports (cases, audit logs)

API Standards: RESTful, JSON payloads, OpenAPI 3.0 spec, rate limiting (1,000 req/min), versioned endpoints (v1, v2, etc.)

Priority: All REST API endpoints are P2.

AXIA
20. Deployment & Infrastructur...

20. Deployment & Infrastructure

20.1 Deployment Models

PriorityModelDescriptionTarget Customer
P1Private CloudDeployed in customer's cloud account (AWS, Azure, GCP), connecting to customer's cloud vendor LLM servicesEnterprise with data sovereignty requirements
P2On-PremisesDeployed in customer's data center with AXIA-provided LLM (no cloud LLM dependency)Regulated industries (finance, government, healthcare)

P1 Cloud LLM Mapping

Customer CloudLLM ServiceNotes
AWSAmazon Bedrock (Claude, Titan)Data stays within customer's AWS account
AzureAzure OpenAI Service (GPT-4)Data stays within customer's Azure tenant
GCPGoogle Vertex AI (Gemini)Data stays within customer's GCP project

The Private Cloud model ensures that all data processing, including LLM inference, occurs within the customer's own cloud environment. No customer data leaves their cloud boundary.

Figure: Deployment Architecture — Side-by-side comparison of Private Cloud (P1) and On-Premises (P2) deployment models showing data boundaries and LLM placement.

AXIA
20.2 Scalability Requirements

20.2 Scalability Requirements

DimensionMinimumTargetMaximum
Concurrent Users101001,000
Monitored Employees50010,000100,000
Events per Day10,0001,000,00050,000,000
Active Cases1005,00050,000
Intel Queries per Day1001,00010,000
Document Storage10 GB1 TB100 TB
AXIA
20.3 Deployment Speed Promise

20.3 Deployment Speed Promise

MilestoneTarget Time
API connection5 minutes
First insights1 hour
Baseline learning24-48 hours
Full protection48 hours
Ongoing tuningAI-automated (continuous)
AXIA
21. Success Criteria

21. Success Criteria

Metrics are organized by phase. P1 metrics focus on case investigation efficiency and AI analysis quality for single-interaction processing with manual engagement approval. P2/P3 metrics layer in behavioral analytics, automation, and organizational intelligence.

21.1 Product Usability Metrics

MetricPhaseTargetMeasurement
Case Investigation TimeP1Reduce by 40% vs. manual DLP reviewTime from case open to resolution
First-Time User OnboardingP1< 15 minutes from login to first case reviewSession analytics
AI Search Success RateP185% of queries return relevant resultsQuery-to-click-through ratio
Intel Action ConversionP160% of recommendations with action buttons are clickedClick-through analytics
Engagement Draft Acceptance RateP1>70% of AI-generated drafts approved with minor or no editsApproval analytics (original AI draft vs. sent version)
Time from AI Recommendation to Operator ActionP1< 5 minutes median for non-escalation actionsTimestamp: AI draft generated → operator confirms

21.2 Value Metrics (AXIA ROI)

MetricPhaseTargetMeasurement
False Positive ReductionP1>70% reduction vs. DLP-only baselineAXIA-validated alerts / total DLP alerts
MTTR ImprovementP1From industry avg (86 days) to < 48 hoursMean time from case creation to resolution
Analyst ProductivityP13x more cases handled per analyst per dayCases resolved / analyst / day
Coverage ExtensionP1Detect 40%+ incidents DLP alone missedAXIA-only detections / total true incidents
Risk Control ScoreP2Improve from ~58 to >90 within 90 daysComposite posture score
Automated Engagement AccuracyP2>90% of auto-sent communications appropriate (no user complaints or retractions)Post-send review sampling

21.3 Deployment Metrics

MetricPhaseTargetMeasurement
Deployment TimeP1< 4 hours average (connect to first case)Time from API connection to first case
Channel Connection TimeP1< 1 hour per channelTime from start to "Connected" status
Time to BaselineP2< 48 hoursTime to establish behavioral baselines

21.4 Trust Metrics

MetricPhaseTargetMeasurement
AI Boundaries ComprehensionP195% of users correctly identify AXIA's limitsPost-onboarding assessment
Legal/Compliance ApprovalP1< 2 weeks from review to deployment sign-offDays from review to approval
Audit Trail CompletenessP1100% of actions logged with full context (including AI recommendation → operator decision chain)Automated verification
AXIA
22. Glossary

22. Glossary

TermDefinition
CaseA risk event identified by AXIA that warrants investigation or action
EntityA person involved in a case (typically an employee)
Structured AnalysisThe What Happened / Why It Matters / Why It Is Unusual framework
Risk Control ScoreComposite metric (0-100) measuring organizational insider risk posture
Signal vs. NoiseRatio of true risk events to total DLP alerts
EngagementAXIA's proactive communication workflow (coaching, notification, escalation)
Severity PlaybookConfigurable rules for which actions are recommended at each severity level
DLP AlertedState where DLP flagged an event but allowed it to proceed
DLP BlockedState where DLP prevented the action
AXIA-Only DetectionEvent identified by AXIA that DLP did not catch
MTTRMean Time to Resolution — average hours from case creation to closure
Behavioral BaselineAI-learned pattern of normal behavior for a role/individual

This is the definitive AXIA product requirements document. For questions or change requests, contact Product Management.
